english: a lion; seń-gei (seng ge'i) kʻri (khri) a throne ('said to be so called from its being supported by golden lions' Will.); seń-mgó (seng mgo) lion's head Glr.; seń-gdoń-ma (seng gdong ma) Sch., = si-ha-mu-ka (si ha mu ka) lion's face, a goddess, Glr.; seń-prug (seng phrug) a lion's whelp, seń-tsań (seng tshang) a lion's den.

S.g, seń-tsér (seng tsher) Wdn., a tree growing on the southern, lower ranges of the Himalayas, having red wood, and a bark which by poor people is used for tea (sdoń-ja (sdong ja)); its sap serves as an officinal drug, Lt.; acc. to Schf. Acacia Catechu.

1. clean, white, cf. skya-seń (skya seng). — 2. Sch.: thin, airy, transparent, not dense or tight, seń-seń (seng seng) id. (Sch.: open, free, roomy, spacious); skyé-bo (skye bo) seń-seń-por (seng seng por) gyur (gyur) they became very thin, lean, pale people, *siń-siń-po* (*sing sing po*), *sińs-po* (*sings po*) W. id.; seń-rás (seng ras) Sch., *siń-yol* (*sing yol*) W. a thin curtain, thin cotton cloth.

pf. bsańs (bsangs), fut. bsań (bsang), bseń (bseng), to lift up, to raise what was hanging down or drooping W. *sań (sang) ċo-ċe (co ce)* or *tań-ċe (tang ce)* to lift up (the eyes, the hands, the dress etc.); sku-kúms (sku kums) bseń-ba-la (bseng ba la) ₒbyon ('byon) (his Reverence) goes to take some recreation Mil.; kʻams (khams) dúb-pa (dub pa) seń-ba (seng ba) to refresh the wearied body Mil.; kʻams (khams) rmúgs-pa (rmugs pa) bseń-bai (bseng ba'i) rluń-ńád (rlung ngad) bracing air; skyo-bsań-ba (skyo bsang ba) to unbend the mind, to divert one's self; skyo-seń-la (skyo seng la) ₒgró-ba ('gro ba) (resp. ₒbyón-pa ('byon pa)) to take a walk, seń-la (seng la) mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) to drive out, to take the air in a carriage Pth.; mya-ńán (mya ngan) bsańs-te (bsangs te) consolatory, giving comfort Pth.
a file, *ċag-sé* (*cag se*) id., *śiń-sé* (*shing se*) a rasp.

resp. pʻyag-sen (phyag sen) or żabs-sén (zhabs sen), nail of a finger or toe; sen-tʻóg (sen thog) a gripe, pinch, nip, twitch; a pinch (of snuff) sén-mo (sen mo) ₒdébs-pa ('debs pa), W. *túb-ċe* (*tub ce*) or *gyáb-ċe* (*gyab ce*) to pinch, squeeze, *sen-ċus* (*sen cus*) *gyáb-ċe* (*gyab ce*) to bore with the knuckles W.; sen-tsám (sen tsam) Sch. as much as may be put on a finger-nail, a small quantity; sen-żó (sen zho) a white spot, such as will sometimes appear on the nails of the fingers.

resp. ཐུགས་ tʻugs (thugs), soul; esp. as power of perception and volition, mind, cf. yid (yid) and blo (blo); sems (sems) na (na) the mind is disturbed, disordered Mńg.; sem (sem) ₒkʻrúgs-pa ('khrugs pa) a mind agitated and troubled by sorrow, affliction, vexation etc., sem (sem) kʻóń-du (khong du) (or kʻóńs-su (khongs su)) ċúd-pa (chud pa) one very much grieved, deeply concerned; sem (sem) ċúń-ba (chung ba) a timid mind, *sem (sem) tsér-ċan* (*tser can*) W. a compassionate disposition, *sem (sem) nyé-mo* (*nye mo*) W. a friendly disposition; sem (sem) gsó-ba (gso ba) B., *sem (sem) so (so) táń-ċe (tang ce)* or *ċúg-ċe (cug ce)* W. to console, comfort, appease; the mind as imaginative faculty, intellectual power, sem (sem) stór-ba (stor ba) to lose one's senses Do.; spirit, kun-gżii (kun gzhi'i) sem (sem) the (eternal) spirit (opp. to ₒbyúń-ba ('byung ba) bżii (bzhi'i) lus (lus) the material, perishable body) Mil.; sém-kyi (sems kyi) spyód-pa (spyod pa) intellectual power, mental faculties Wdń.; dńós-po (dngos po) tʻams-ċád (thams cad) rań-gi (rang gi) sems (sems) yin-te (yin te) 'as things with me are only mind', i.e. as they exist only in my mind, in my imagination Thgr., cf. Was. (136); sém-la (sems la) ma (ma) soń (song) 'it did not enter his mind', he had no mind, did not like W.; sems (sems) ₒgyur ('gyur) (his) mind is changed, sems (sems) sgyur-ba (sgyur ba) to change one's mind, μετανοεῖν; bzód-pai (bzod pa'i) sems (sems) patient indurance, fortitude, constancy; gnod-sems (gnod sems) malice; śes-ráb (shes rab) dań (dang) ldań-pai (ldan pa'i) sems (sems) wisdom, knowledge; sems (sems) skyéd-pa (skyed pa), c. genit., to suffer thoughts or inclinations to rise in one's mind, as e.g. ₒdod-ċágs-kyi ('dod chags kyi) libidinous (thoughts), frq.; also: to nourish, indulge (desires, passions), to give way to them; often used for our reflective verbs: sems (sems) smád-pa (smad pa) to humble one's self (mi (mi) żig-la (zhig la) before a person); also: bdág-gi (bdag gi) séms-la (sems la) smad (smad) he blamed, scolded, himself Dzl. 7S, 3, cf. lus (lus). Comp. séms-mkʻan (sems mkhan) intelligent, sensible, séms-mkʻan (sems mkhan) mi (mi) gċig (gcig) kyań (kyang) ma (ma) byuń (byung) not one sensible person was present Glr. — sems-ₒkʻrál (sems 'khral) a mind afflicted, painfully agitated Sch. — sems-ċan (sems can) animated being, man, animal, very frq.; séms-ċan (sems can) dań (dang) ldań-pa (ldan pa) being with child, pregnant; sems-ċan-dmyál-ba (sems can dmyal ba) = dmyál-ba (dmyal ba)
